---
title: "Power_curve"
author: "Galen O'Shea-Stone"
date: "2025-08-11"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# install.packages("pwr")
library(pwr)

effect_sizes <- c(0.5, 0.8, 1.2, 2.0)
n_vals <- 2:20
alpha <- 0.05

df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(effect_sizes, function(d){
  data.frame(
    n_per_group = n_vals,
    effect_size_d = d,
    power = sapply(n_vals, function(n){
      # pwr.t.test uses total n for two-sample: n = n1 + n2
      out <- pwr.t.test(d=d, n=2*n, sig.level=alpha, type="two.sample", alternative="two.sided")
      out$power
    })
  )
}))

print(head(df))

# plot
plot(NULL, xlim=range(n_vals), ylim=c(0,1), xlab="Sample size per group",
     ylab="Power (two-sample t-test, α=0.05)",
     main="Statistical Power vs. Sample Size per Group")
cols <- 1:length(effect_sizes)
for (i in seq_along(effect_sizes)) {
  d <- effect_sizes[i]
  sub <- df[df$effect_size_d==d, ]
  lines(sub$n_per_group, sub$power, type="l", lwd=2, col=cols[i])
}
abline(h=0.8, lty=2)
abline(v=3, lty=3)
legend("bottomright", legend=paste0("d=", effect_sizes),
       col=cols, lwd=2, bty="n")



```
